burden.dalyPerCapita: low mortality; significant QOL and work impairment

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Rome IV criteria for IBS-D; exclude alarm features and inflammatory bowel disease

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Millions of adults across the EU
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High with dietitian support and multimodal therapy

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Clinical diagnosis with access to fecal calprotectin to rule out IBD
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Dietary modification (low FODMAP), antispasmodics, rifaximin, loperamide; CBT when available
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Variation in dietitian access across countries
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Strong guideline support and multimodal care pathways
